---
title: ""
output: 
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
---
<style>
.html-widget {
    width: 910px !important;
    height: 975px !important;
}
body { margin: 0; }
</style>

```{r, include=FALSE, error=TRUE}

# load libraries and test data
library(here)
library(stringr)

source("W:\\RDA Team\\R\\credentials_source.R")
source(here("MOSAIC", "charts", "chart_style_vars.R"))
#source("W:/Project/RACE COUNTS/2025_v7/RC_Github/LF/RaceCounts/MOSAIC/charts/chart_style_vars.R")
con <- connect_to_db("mosaic")


knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

```


```{r}
##### DOCUMENTATION (can skip to next section for indicator inputs) #####
### Requirements for chart behavior: Toggling between rate and counts
# rate_order - created automatically by script; helps put bars in the preferred order for rate view
# count_order - created automatically by script; helps put bars in the preferred order for count view

### Requirements: Chart Metadata (must be provided per indicator)
# data_source - e.g., "US Department of Health and Human Services, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), National Center for Health Statistics, Division of Vital Statistics, CDC WONDER Online Database (2016-2023)"

# data_description - e.g., "The number of infants born weighing less than 2,500 grams or about 5 lbs 8 oz per 100 live births by race/ethnicity of birthing parent. Estimates for racial groups with fewer than ten births or areas with fewer than 100,000 people were excluded to protect privacy. SWANA data includes only foreign-born birthing parents."

# exception: methodology_link is controlled by setting a global variable in chart_style_vars
# methodology_link - e.g., "<a href='https://catalystcalifornia.github.io/RaceCounts/Methodology/Indicator_Methodology_CountyState.html' target='_blank'>Full Methodology</a>"

# chart_title - e.g., "Low Birthweight by Race/Ethnicity"

# indicator_asbest - e.g., "min" or "max"

# total_rate - single variable to establish vertical plotline; total/state rate  of the indicator  

### Requirements: Chart data / Dataframe structure
# The ideal shape of the indicator table is long - with columns for group, subgroup, rate, count. It should have both the mosaic group (asian and/or nhpi and total which should include state (subgroup = total) and the RC race subgroups). It should also have Asian/NHPI as a subgroup under total group
# Ideally rates are already rounded in this table or the metadata table includes this (or we make all round to 1 decimal place)
# Ideally it or an accompanying metadata table includes the asbest value for the indicator.
# additional metadata fields include round rate unless we want all indicators to round to one place
# group - name of column with aggregate group name (e.g., Asian, NHPI, Total) - should be all caps
# subgroup - name of column with subgroup names (Chinese, Indian, etc.) - should be all caps
# rate - name of column with indicator raced rates
# raw - name of column with indicator raced counts
```

```{r}
##### Prep: Get data (must change for each indicator) #####
options(scipen = 999)

# Get indicator df
indicator_wide <- dbGetQuery(con, paste0("SELECT * FROM v7.", pop_table, " WHERE geoid = '06'"))
table_name <- str_sub(pop_table, -6)

# 1. Pivot raw columns (ignores the 'name' column)
df_raw <- indicator_wide %>%
  select(geoid, name, geolevel, matches(paste0(table_name,"_\\d+e$"))) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches(paste0(table_name,"_\\d+e$")),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "raw"
  )

# 2. Pivot rate columns (ignores the 'name' column)
df_rate <- indicator_wide %>%
  select(geoid, name, geolevel, matches(paste0(table_name,"_\\d+_pct$"))) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches(paste0(table_name,"_\\d+_pct$")),
    names_to = "pct_name",
    values_to = "rate"
  ) %>%
  # Convert 'b02018_001_pct' to 'b02018_001e' to match df_raw keys
  mutate(variable = paste0(sub("_pct$", "", pct_name), "e")) %>%
  select(-pct_name)

# 3. Combine them perfectly side-by-side
long_df <- left_join(df_raw, df_rate, by = c("geoid", "name", "geolevel", "variable"))

# Set metadata - see documentation above for examples
indicator_name <- str_sub(table_name, -6)
indicator_asbest <- indicator_metadata %>% filter(api_name == indicator_name) %>% select(arei_best) %>% pull()
data_source <- indicator_metadata %>% filter(api_name == indicator_name) %>% select(data_source) %>% pull()
data_description <- indicator_metadata %>% filter(api_name == indicator_name) %>% select(methodology) %>% pull()
chart_title <- indicator_metadata %>% filter(api_name == indicator_name) %>% select(bar_chart_header) %>% pull()
rate_rounding <- indicator_metadata %>% filter(api_name == indicator_name) %>% select(rate_rounding) %>% pull() %>% as.integer()
raw_rounding <- indicator_metadata %>% filter(api_name == indicator_name) %>% select(raw_rounding) %>% pull() %>% as.integer()

##### Get Labels #####
# 1. Fetch the 2024 5-year ACS metadata dictionary
acs_metadata <- load_variables(2024, "acs5", cache = TRUE)

# 2. Filter down to Table B02018 and format the names to end in 'e'
subgroup_labels <- acs_metadata %>%
  filter(str_starts(name, paste0(toupper(indicator_name),"_"))) %>%
  mutate(
    # Format the names to match your data (e.g., 'B02018_007' -> 'b02018_007e')
    variable = paste0(tolower(name), "e"),
    
    # Extract only the characters after the absolute last '!!' marker
    clean_label = str_extract(label, "[^!]+$"),
    
    # Optional cleanup: Trim any accidental whitespace
    clean_label = str_trim(clean_label)
  ) %>%
  select(variable, subgroup = clean_label)

# 3. Merge the clean metadata labels into your long dataframe
indicator_df <- long_df %>%
  left_join(subgroup_labels, by = "variable") %>%
  # Position the new 'label' column directly after the 'variable' column
  relocate(subgroup, .after = variable)


# need to filter out total raw/rate
indicator_df <- indicator_df %>%
  filter(!grepl("_001e", variable))

# Don't change - pulled in from chart_style_vars
methodology_link <- sprintf("<a href='%s' target='_blank'>Full Methodology</a>", mosaic_methodology_link)

```

```{r}
##### Prep: Edit data for chart (don't change) #####

# use indicator best direction to arrange "shared struggle" groups at top
if(indicator_asbest=="min") {
  indicator_df <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(desc(rate)) %>%
    mutate(rate = round(rate, rate_rounding),
           raw = round(raw, raw_rounding))
    
  # Create ordered categories for RATES (descending by rate)
  rate_order <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(desc(rate)) %>%
    pull(subgroup)

  # Create ordered categories for COUNTS (descending by count)
  count_order <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(desc(raw)) %>%
    pull(subgroup)
  
} else{
  indicator_df <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(rate) %>%
    mutate(rate = round(rate, rate_rounding),
           raw = round(raw, raw_rounding))
  
  # Create ordered categories for RATES (descending by rate)
  rate_order <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(rate) %>%
    pull(subgroup)

  # Create ordered categories for COUNTS (descending by count)
  count_order <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(raw) %>%
    pull(subgroup)
}

# Create series data for RATES with color
data_points_rates <- map(1:nrow(indicator_df), function(i) {
  x_index <- which(rate_order == indicator_df$subgroup[i]) - 1
  list(
    x = unname(x_index),
    y = indicator_df$rate[i],
    name = indicator_df$subgroup[i]
  )
})

series_data_rates <- list(list(
  label = "rates",
  data  = data_points_rates,
  id    = "rates",
  visible = FALSE,
  color = rc_orange
))


# Create series data for COUNTS with color
data_points_counts <- map(1:nrow(indicator_df), function(i) {
  x_index <- which(count_order == indicator_df$subgroup[i]) - 1
  list(
    x = unname(x_index),
    y = indicator_df$raw[i],
    name = indicator_df$subgroup[i]
  )
})

series_data_counts <- list(list(
  label   = "counts",
  data    = data_points_counts,
  id      = "counts",
  visible = TRUE,
  color   = rc_orange
))

```

```{r}
##### Chart: Highcharts bar (don't change) ##### 
hc <- highchart() %>%
  hc_chart(
    type = "bar",
    reflow = TRUE,
    events = list(
      load = htmlwidgets::JS(sprintf(paste0("
        function() {
          var chart = this;
          var rateOrder = %s;
          var countOrder = %s;
          
          // Create toggle button
          chart.customToggleButton = chart.renderer.button(
            'Show Rates',
            chart.chartWidth - 106,           
            chart.chartHeight / 2 - 50,       
            function() {
              var showingRates = true;
              
              // Check if rates are currently visible
              chart.series.forEach(function(s) {
                if (s.userOptions.id && s.userOptions.id === 'rates') {
                  showingRates = s.visible;
                }
              });
              
              // Toggle all series and update x-axis
              chart.series.forEach(function(s) {
                if (s.userOptions.id) {
                  if (showingRates) {
                    if (s.userOptions.id === 'rates') {
                      s.update({showInLegend: false}, false);
                      s.hide();
                    } else if (s.userOptions.id === 'counts') {
                      s.update({showInLegend: true}, false);
                      s.show();
                    }
                  } else {
                    if (s.userOptions.id === 'counts') {
                      s.update({showInLegend: false}, false);
                      s.hide();
                    } else if (s.userOptions.id === 'rates') {
                      s.update({showInLegend: true}, false);
                      s.show();
                    }
                  }
                }
              });
              
              if (showingRates) {
                chart.customToggleButton.attr({text: 'Show Rates'});
                chart.yAxis[0].setTitle({ text: 'COUNT' }, false); 
                chart.xAxis[0].update({categories: countOrder}, false);
                chart.update({
                  plotOptions: {
                    bar: {
                      dataLabels: {
                        formatter: function() {
                          return Highcharts.numberFormat(this.y, 0, '.', ',');
                        }
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }, true);
                
              } else {
                chart.customToggleButton.attr({text: 'Show Counts'});
                chart.yAxis[0].setTitle({ text: 'RATE' }, false); 
                chart.xAxis[0].update({categories: rateOrder}, false);
                chart.update({
                  plotOptions: {
                    bar: {
                      dataLabels: {
                        formatter: function() {
                          return this.y;
                        }
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }, true);
              } 
            },
            {
              fill: '", rc_blue, "',
              stroke: '", rc_blue, "',
              style: { color: '", rc_white, "' },
              'stroke-width': 1,
              r: 3,
              padding: 8
            },
            {
              fill: '", rc_blue, "',
              style: { color: '", rc_white, "' }
            },
            {
              fill: '", rc_blue, "',
              style: { color: '", rc_white, "' }
            }
          )
          .css({
            color: '", rc_white, "'
          })
          .attr({ zIndex: 7 })
          .add();
          
          Highcharts.addEvent(chart, 'redraw', function() {
            if (chart.customToggleButton) {
              var buttonWidth = chart.customToggleButton.getBBox().width;
              var rightEdge = chart.chartWidth - chart.spacing[1];
              chart.customToggleButton.attr({
                x: rightEdge - buttonWidth
              });
            }
          });
          
          var buttonWidth = chart.customToggleButton.getBBox().width;
          var rightEdge = chart.chartWidth - chart.spacing[1];
          chart.customToggleButton.attr({
            x: rightEdge - buttonWidth
          });
        }
      "),
      jsonlite::toJSON(rate_order),
      jsonlite::toJSON(count_order))
      ))) %>%
  hc_plotOptions(
    bar = list(
      dataLabels = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        align = "right",
        inside = FALSE,
        formatter = JS("function() { return Highcharts.numberFormat(this.y, 0, '.', ','); }"), 
        style = list(color = "black")),
      pointWidth = 20,
      grouping = FALSE,
      pointPadding = 0,
      groupPadding = 0.1),
    series = list(enableMouseTracking = FALSE),
    clip = FALSE) %>%
  hc_xAxis(
    categories = count_order,
    labels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      useHTML = TRUE,            # Required for complex CSS formatting (like word-break)
      formatter = JS("
        function() {
          // Explicitly wrap the text inside a fixed-width, wrapping div
          return '<div style=\"width: 100px; white-space: normal; word-wrap: break-word; text-align: right;\">' + this.value + '</div>';
        }
      ")),
    gridLineWidth = 1,
    gridLineColor = '#e6e6e6') %>%
  hc_yAxis(
    title = list(text = "COUNT"),
    labels = list(enabled = FALSE),
    gridLineWidth = 0,
    offset = 0
  ) %>%
  hc_legend(
    enabled = TRUE,
    verticalAlign = "top",
    margin = 20
  ) %>%
  hc_tooltip(enabled = FALSE) %>%
  hc_title(
    text = chart_title,
    align = "center") %>% 
  hc_subtitle(
    text = "Toggle between rates and counts using the button below",
    align = "center") %>%
  hc_caption(
    text = paste("<b>About the data:", data_source, "</b>", data_description, methodology_link),
    useHTML = TRUE,
    align = "left"
  ) %>%
  hc_add_series_list(series_data_rates) %>%
  hc_add_series_list(series_data_counts) %>%
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) %>%
  hc_add_theme(custom_theme) %>%
  hc_responsive(
  rules = list(
    list(
      condition = list(maxWidth = breakpoint_maxwidth),
      chartOptions = list(
        caption = list(
          text = paste("<b>Source:</b>", data_source, methodology_link)
        )
      )
    )
  )
)




# Wrap with constrained width
htmltools::tagList(
  htmltools::tags$div(
    class = "chart-wrapper",
    style = "max-width: 1200px; width: 100%; margin: 0 auto;",
    htmltools::tags$div(
      class = "chart-container",
      style = "height: auto; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #ccc;",
      hc
    )
  ),
  htmltools::tags$script(HTML("
    function resizeChart() {
    if (typeof Highcharts !== 'undefined' && Highcharts.charts.length > 0) {
      Highcharts.charts.forEach(function(chart) {
        if (chart) {
          // Check if we're inside an iframe
          var inIframe = window.self !== window.top;
          
          var targetWidth;
          if (inIframe) {
            // In iframe: use full window width
            targetWidth = window.innerWidth;
          } else {
            // Standalone: use chart wrapper width
            var wrapper = document.querySelector('.chart-wrapper');
            targetWidth = wrapper ? wrapper.offsetWidth - 40 : window.innerWidth;
          }
          
          chart.setSize(targetWidth, null, false);
        }
      });
    }
  }
    
    setTimeout(resizeChart, 100);
    window.addEventListener('resize', resizeChart);
  "))
)

```
